cards.back: A flowchart that represents a set of possible decisions and their possible consequences
A class of decision problems for which it is unknown whether every problem in the class can be solved exactly in polynomial time
A class of decision problems for which a proposed solution can be checked in polynomial time, but a proposed solution may not always be correct
A decision problem that is NP-complete, meaning it is in NP and every problem in NP can be reduced to it
A process of transforming one problem into another, where the transformed problem is at least as hard as the original problem
